certification_status_detail: Full calibration evidence for gpu-medium-v2. Positive control (RTX 4070 Ti SUPER, 16 GB, T3) staged through protocol with 22 challenges / 92 clean stages. Negative control (RTX 4070 SUPER, 12 GB, T2) benchmarked; compute-competitive (slowdown 1.33, threshold 2.0). VRAM wall confirmed below T2 floor (8 GB devices OOM on 8.64 GB working set). Precompute budget admissible for early-to-growth deployment.

gate_closure.gate_1_pre_calibration: CLOSED (2026-03-18) — benchmark on RTX 4070 Ti SUPER: 118.27 ms/stage total, 68.01 ms GPU, < 3% variance
gate_closure.gate_2_hardware_qualification: CLOSED (2026-03-18) — positive: RTX 4070 Ti SUPER (T3, 16 GB), negative: RTX 4070 SUPER (T2, 12 GB). Same architecture (Ada Lovelace), compute_slowdown=1.33 < 2.0 threshold. gpu-large-v2 OOM on negative confirms VRAM tier boundary.
gate_closure.gate_3_full_timing_calibration: CLOSED (2026-03-19) — 22 challenges through staging protocol, 110 total stages, 92 clean stages (no GPU contention). Server-measured p99=157ms (clean). Heavy-tail ratio (clean)=1.15 PASS.
gate_closure.gate_4_precompute_budget: CLOSED (2026-03-18) — T_stage=14,730ms on Intel Ultra 9 285K. 5.70 sets/min at parallelism=7. Admissible for I_target <= 5.
gate_closure.gate_6_profile_row_insertion: CLOSED (2026-03-19) — profile_id=prod-gpu-medium-v2, class_id=202, protocol_version=2 inserted into compute_resource_class_profiles
gate_closure.gate_7_post_insertion_validation: OPEN — pending positive/negative control verification against production profile

errata_reference: See ERRATUM-001-M-calculation.md for M value correction. Frozen calibration plan not rewritten.

proposed_profile.target_working_set_description: M x N x 4 = 524288 x 4096 x 4 = 8,589,934,592 bytes = 8.59 GB. Requires ~8.64 GB GPU memory including X/Y vectors and scratch buffers.

pre_calibration.hardware_description: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti SUPER, 16 GB GDDR6X, Ada Lovelace (AD103), T3 floor
pre_calibration.dimension_selection_notes: M=524,288 derived from: floor(8 GB / (4096 x 4)) = 524,288. Produces 8.59 GB A matrix. K=8 per spec tractability constraint. mix_rounds=2 (gpu-medium). N=4096 per Phase 2A consistency.

positive_control.tier_justification: 16 GB VRAM = T3 floor (13-20 GB range)
positive_control.contamination_note: 18/110 stages (16%) had GPU preemption spikes from concurrent precompute worker sharing the GPU. These are not production-representative (precompute runs on CPU only). Clean signal (92 stages) is authoritative.
positive_control.timing_authority: Server-measured: checkpoint_received_at - stage_issued_at. Includes network RTT, GPU compute, worker serialization. Excludes server-side validation.
positive_control.staging_overhead_explanation: staging p50 (136ms) - benchmark total (118ms) = ~18ms attributable to network RTT + JSON serialization
positive_control.selection_notes: RTX 4070 Ti SUPER is T3 floor (16 GB). Ada Lovelace. Same architecture as negative control.

negative_control.tier_justification: 12 GB VRAM = T2 ceiling (7-12 GB range)

compute_competitiveness.throughput_ratio_test_run: gpu-medium-v2 benchmark: positive 118.27ms vs negative 197.74ms per stage total
compute_competitiveness.justification: bandwidth ratio 504/672 = 0.75 explains observed 1.33x slowdown. No class-level compute gap.

negative_control.failure_mode: DOES NOT FAIL — gpu-medium-v2 working set (8.64 GB) fits on 12 GB device. Negative control runs the workload successfully but slower.
negative_control.selection_notes: RTX 4070 SUPER is T2 ceiling (12 GB). Same architecture as positive control. gpu-medium-v2 FITS (no OOM). gpu-large-v2 OOMs — confirms VRAM tier boundary at 12.96 GB working set.

separation_margin.separation_type_detail: gpu-medium-v2 working set is 8.64 GB. Devices with < ~9 GB usable VRAM (all T1, low-T2 with 8 GB) OOM. The T2 ceiling (12 GB) negative control runs the workload — separation at T2/T3 boundary is timing-based, not OOM-based. True binary VRAM wall is at ~8 GB device level.
separation_margin.heavy_tail_note: Clean signal (no GPU contention) ratio 1.15 PASS. All-stages ratio 62.955 caused by GPU preemption from concurrent precompute worker — not production-representative.
separation_margin.deadline_derivation: p99 (157ms) x 3.2 = 502ms, rounded to 500ms. Provides 2.3x headroom over estimated negative control staging (216ms).
separation_margin.false_negative_explanation: deadline (500ms) - positive p99 (157ms) = 343ms buffer. Even under network jitter, legitimate T3 devices have wide margin.
separation_margin.negative_ooms_at_which_class: gpu-large-v2 (12.96 GB working set on 12 GB device)
separation_margin.vram_wall_detail: T1 (6 GB) OOMs on 8.64 GB. Low-T2 (8 GB, e.g. RTX 4060 8 GB) OOMs on 8.64 GB. High-T2 (12 GB) and T3 (16+ GB) fit.
separation_margin.admissibility_notes: Working set is 8.59 GB = 50% of 16 GB positive control VRAM (below 65% drift threshold). Clean heavy-tail ratio 1.15. Deadline at 500ms provides adequate headroom for all devices that can fit the working set.

precompute_budget.target_issuance_rate_challenges_per_minu: DEPLOYMENT DECISION — see SLA scenarios below

sla_scenarios.label: Scale phase (200+ nodes)
sla_scenarios.note: gpu-medium-v2 precompute is NOT admissible at I=5/min. Larger working set (8.24 GB RSS per instance) limits parallelism to 7 vs 14 for gpu-small-v2. Scale-phase deployment requires either more RAM or dedicated precompute infrastructure.

precompute_budget.notes: Memory-limited at 7 parallel instances (8.24 GB each). Admissible for early and growth deployment. Scale phase (I=5/min) exceeds single-server precompute capacity — acknowledged limitation.

deadline_derivation.source: Server-measured staging timing, 92 clean stages (GPU contention excluded)
deadline_derivation.stage_deadline_derivation: 157ms x 3.2 = 502ms → 500ms. Accommodates T2 devices (estimated staging ~216ms, headroom 2.3x) and RTX 3060 12 GB (estimated ~193ms, headroom 2.6x). T1 devices (6 GB) OOM — no deadline concern.
deadline_derivation.first_progress_derivation: 10 seconds. 63.7x headroom over positive p99. Allows network establishment and GPU context warm-up for first stage. Production workers have persistent GPU context.
deadline_derivation.completion_derivation: 10 seconds for all 5 stages. 12.7x headroom over positive 5-stage p99 total (785ms). 9.3x over estimated T2 ceiling total (1080ms). Generous for sustained per-stage jitter.

admissibility_summary.gate_1_pre_calibration: CLOSED — benchmark on RTX 4070 Ti SUPER (T3 floor): 118.27ms/stage, <3% variance, 5 runs. Benchmark artifact: gpu-benchmark-rtx4070ti-super.json
admissibility_summary.gate_2_hardware_qualification: CLOSED — positive (RTX 4070 Ti SUPER, 16 GB, T3) + negative (RTX 4070 SUPER, 12 GB, T2). Same architecture (Ada Lovelace). compute_slowdown=1.33 < 2.0. gpu-large-v2 OOM on negative confirms tier boundary.
admissibility_summary.gate_3_full_timing_calibration: CLOSED — 22 challenges, 110 stages (92 clean) through staging protocol. Server-measured: p50=136ms, p99=157ms, σ=9ms (clean). Heavy-tail ratio 1.15 PASS.
admissibility_summary.gate_4_precompute_budget: CLOSED — T_stage=14,730ms, parallelism=7, throughput=5.70 sets/min. Admissible for I<=2 with headroom. I=5 NOT admissible (acknowledged).
admissibility_summary.gate_7_post_insertion_validation: OPEN — pending positive/negative control verification against production profile
admissibility_summary.overall: CERTIFIED (Gates 1-6 closed). Profile inserted. Pending post-insertion validation (Gate 7).
